title: Light Quick Malaysian Shungiku Satay
description: Sweet and savory, this light Malaysian dish is perfect for a quick fix dinner. Featuring shungiku, a Asian green, and a sweet lacquer glaze, this pescatarian-friendly recipe is a flavorful and refreshing twist on traditional satay.

ingredients.name: Shungiku
ingredients.notes: Stem and slice the shungiku into thin strips.

ingredients.name: Lacquer Glaze
ingredients.notes: You can find lacquer glaze in most Asian grocery stores or make your own by mixing equal parts honey and soy sauce.

ingredients.name: Panko Breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: To make this dish gluten-free, use gluten-free panko breadcrumbs.

ingredients.name: Coconut Oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral-tasting oil like coconut oil for grilling.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Mince the ginger and mix with the lacquer glaze for a bold flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic and mix with the lacquer glaze for a pungent flavor.

ingredients.name: Fish Sauce
ingredients.notes: This is a key component of the lacquer glaze, so don't skip it!

ingredients.name: Brown Sugar
ingredients.notes: Balance out the savory flavors with a touch of sweetness.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: This adds depth and umami to the dish.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground black pepper for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Chili Flakes
ingredients.notes: Add a touch of heat to your satay (optional)

ingredients.name: Shrimp
ingredients.notes: Peel and de-vein the shrimp before grilling.

ingredients.name: Toasted Coconut Flakes
ingredients.notes: Sprinkle toasted coconut flakes on top of the satay for added flavor and texture.

instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat. Thread the shrimp and shungiku onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Brush the panko breadcrumbs onto the shrimp and shungiku for a crispy coating.
Mix the lacquer glaze ingredients together and brush onto the skewers.
Grill the skewers for 2-3 minutes per side, or until the shrimp are pink and the shungiku is tender.
Serve the satay hot, garnished with toasted coconut flakes and a sprinkle of sesame seeds.
